title: Toddler Farmhouse Bed (Crib Mattress)
summary: A low farmhouse toddler bed sized for a crib mattress, with planked headboard and footboard panels and an optional arched header. All 1x boards.
description:
A toddler's first big-kid bed, sized to reuse the crib mattress you already own. It's a scaled-down farmhouse bed — planked headboard and footboard panels framed by built-up legs, an optional arched header up top, and a simple slat-on-cleats platform low to the ground so a toddler can climb in and out safely.

Everything is 1x stock, joined with glue, 1-1/4" nails, and 2" screws. The panels are the signature: 1x4 boards ganged into a field with 1x3 side pieces, trimmed top and bottom. The legs are built up from an inside piece and an outside full-length piece so they read as solid, and the header can be arched (a 1x6 you shape) or left square (swap in a 1x2) — your call.

Two details matter for a bed. The side-rail notches in the leg bottoms: confirm your 1x6 is exactly 5-1/2" wide before you cut the notch, or the rails won't seat flush. And the cleats — 1x2 strips run inside the side rails to carry the 18 slats. Screw the slats down so an active toddler can't kick them loose. Keep it low and round every edge.

materials.name: 1x3, 8 ft
materials.note: Panel side pieces and top pieces.

materials.name: 1x6, 8 ft
materials.note: Header, side rails, endplate. Adjust if not cutting an arch.

cutList.note: Cut 2 if not doing an arch.
Arch to taste; use a 1x2 if not arching.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut six headboard center panel at 18-1/2" and six footboard center panel at 9" from the 1x4. Cut two headboard panel sides at 18-1/2", one headboard top cap at 30", two footboard panel sides at 9" and one footboard top cap at 30" from the 1x3. Cut one headboard bottom trim at 26", two headboard inside legs at 18-1/2", two headboard full legs at 30", one headboard top of panel/legs at 29", two headboard inside bottom legs at 6", two footboard trim at 26", four footboard legs at 15", one footboard top of panel/legs at 29", two cleats at 53-1/2" and 18 slats at 26" from the 1x2. Cut one headboard header at 26", two side rails at 54-1/2" and one endplate at 26" from the 1x6. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to two 8 ft 1x4, two 8 ft 1x3, ten 8 ft 1x2 and two 8 ft 1x6. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Build the headboard panel
steps.body: Fasten the 1x4 center panel boards to the bottom trim piece, using 1-1/4" nails and glue. Add the 1x3 side pieces to frame the field. This is the planked panel that forms the middle of the headboard.

steps.title: Build up the headboard legs
steps.body: Fasten the 18-1/2" inside leg pieces to the sides of the center panel with 2" screws, driving into the 1x3 panel sides and the bottom trim only; the header is not fitted until the next step, so leave that upper connection open for now. Then glue and nail the 30" full outside leg pieces onto the inside legs with 1-1/4" brads. Building the legs up in two layers makes them read solid, but keep the edges flush while you nail or the stacked leg will need extra sanding before finish.

steps.title: Cap the headboard and shape the header
steps.body: Fasten the top piece across the headboard with 2" nails, measuring and cutting to fit, and add the top cap. Arch the 1x6 header with a jigsaw if you want the arched look, or leave it square with a 1x2. Add the inside bottom leg pieces.

steps.title: Build the footboard
steps.body: Build the footboard panel exactly as you built the headboard panel — 1x4 field, 1x3 sides, trim — then attach the inside legs with screws, the outer legs with nails, and cap the top. It's the same sequence at a shorter height.

steps.title: Notch the leg bottoms for the rails
steps.body: Measure your 1x6 to confirm it's exactly 5-1/2" wide, then notch the leg bottoms for the side rails per the diagram. Getting the notch to match the actual rail width is what lets the rails seat flush. Fasten with 1-1/4" nails and glue.

steps.title: Assemble the frame
steps.body: Fasten the side rails to the endplate with 2" screws, then fasten the endplate to the footboard with 1-1/4" screws, and tie the rails to the headboard. This forms the bed frame between the two ends.

steps.title: Add cleats and slats
steps.body: Fasten the 1x2 cleats to the inside of the side rails with glue and 1-1/4" nails. Lay the 18 slats across the cleats and screw them down — screwing (not just resting) the slats keeps an active toddler from kicking them loose. Fill nail holes, sand every edge round, and finish with a kid-safe finish.

images.alt: Low farmhouse toddler bed with a planked, arched headboard sized for a crib mattress
